titleInfo.title: Notes on the Meaning of the Amitābha Sutra

mods.abstract: Commentary to T#366. First a general introduction on Amitābha and his vows, rebirth in the West, and Śākyamuni’s role in teaching this. Then the sutra title is explained, 阿彌陀 and 經. Next, the sutra in general is explained or classified according to five aspects: 1. 釋名 2.辨體 3.宗教 4.力用 5.教相. Now follows the actual discussion of the sutra. Zhiyi says it has three parts: A preface 序, the actual sermon 正[說], and a part about the spreading of the sutra 流通. This corresponds to the modern understanding of introductio, main part, and end. Zhiyi always quotes the first characters of a certain passage he is about to illuminate; he does not quote the sutra sentence by sentence or word by word, as this is only a short commentary. The actual sermon, the main part [II], he divides in two large parts 正說為二: 初 and 次, first II.1 and second II.2. The first is an explanation about the two kinds of fruits the other Buddha bases his orthodox [teaching] 初明彼佛依正二果，the second an exhortation to seek rebirth 次權物往生。 The first part is again divided in two parts, a former 前 II.1.1, displaying the pure land, and a latter 後 II.1.2, explaining the manifested [beings]. Then it gets harder to follow all of the above divisions. However, the terms 總、別、結 seem to be standrad term for structuring arguments, corresponding to generally, particularly,resulting in.

titleInfo.title: New edition of the Buddhist canon in the Taishō era
